texts: ow to to stop looping if name 'Rakshith' found in xml dom parsing. And in simple xml parser we can directly access the nth element like $xml->Person[0]->Name. But in XML dom, we need to parse in foreach itself so far i know. Is there any way like this that would suit my requirement. Thanks in advance A: If you're are using DOM. You can break out of the loop once you find the name 'Rakshith' like this: ```php $xml = new DOMDocument(); $xml->loadXML(yourxmlstringhere); $persons = $xml->getElementsByTagName('Person'); foreach ($persons as $person) { $name = $person->getElementsByTagName('Name')->item(0)->textContent; if ($name == 'Rakshith') { break; } } // $person now contains the node for 'Rakshith' ``` This way, as soon as the 'Rakshith' name node is found, the loop is broken, and $person is set to the node for 'Rakshith' for further processing, if needed. This is the way to do the same thing as $xml->Person[0]->Name, as you mentioned for the SimpleXML, in the DOM way. Remember to replace 'yourxmlstringhere' in the $xml->loadXML() method, to the actual XML you want to parse. This is the same as the SimpleXML way, as you mentioned, $xml->Person[0]->Name, in the DOM way. Remember, in DOM, you cannot access the XML elements as you access the elements in the SimpleXML, as SimpleXML is more object-oriented. DOM is more low-level, in the way, you access the elements. Remember, the DOM is more low-level, in the way, you access the elements, as you cannot access the XML elements as you access the elements in the SimpleXML, as SimpleXML is more object-oriented. MMORPG (Massive Multi-Player Online RolePlaying Game) has become the norm rather than the exception in recent years with games like World of Warcraft leading the charge. The pay-to-play model, however, is one that I have always shied away from due to my belief that gaming should be accessible and affordable for everyone. My stance on this issue became even more pronounced when I discovered a game called "The Elder Scrolls Online" (TESO). TESO was developed by Bethesda Game Studios, the same team behind some of the most beloved RPG series in history like The Elder Scroll Series. This made me incredibly

he sales agreement on August 19 that no actual permit for additional spaces had been granted, and that plaintiff nevertheless determined to proceed with the purchase. We also agree with the trial court that plaintiff is not in a position to complain about the condition of the sewer system. Immediately after he took possession of the property on August 1, plaintiff discovered that the sewer system was not operating adequately. The situation was discussed with the defendants and, on August 19, an agreement was signed which provided that defendants would contribute up to $750 for the necessary repairs. After having freely entered into this agreement, plaintiff is not *253 in a position to assert the same defect as a basis for rescission. Plaintiff, however, relies primarily on the representation that defendants' gross income from the property for 1973 was just over $33,000. This information was supplied to plaintiff in a prospectus which he received before he signed the earnest money agreement. In contrast, defendants' income tax return for 1973, which was introduced at trial, showed a gross income of only $23,000. Between the time that plaintiff signed the earnest money receipt and the execution of the sales agreement, plaintiff requested and received additional information from defendants' realtor regarding defendants' income for 1973. On July 6, 1974, plaintiff received a letter showing rental charged for the various trailer spaces. On July 16, after a further request, plaintiff received from the realtor an additional breakdown of the income for 1973. This information was consistent with the original representation as to gross income. However, the letter of transmittal contained the following admonition: "Enclosed find the breakdown on the individual sources of income as tendered by Mr. Fantus. Along these same lines of thought may we suggest that you come up and inspect the books personally and to your satisfaction as Nielsen Realty will not assume responsibility for the accuracy of these figures." Plaintiff made no effort to examine the records before signing the

He is also the editor of the "Energy Advantage" advisory service.] In last week's State of the Union address, U.S. President Barack Obama issued a national challenge: Take the 11.5% of the U.S. electricity that emanates from clean-energy sources and boost it to 80% by 2035. As with any game-changing direction – landing a man on the moon in a decade, bringing an end to the Cold War, curing cancer, or weaning our economy off of coal and crude oil – leaders such as President Obamashould explain why they think what's been tried before is no longer feasible (or even beneficial). In all fairness…there isn't. Since February 1st - just two months ago- investment bankers Lehman Brothers now claims the United States added more clean energy than it had ever envisioned was possible by this point in history. It set out with a goal of adding at least $50 billion worth before then, and hit its objective – going beyond$110 Billion. So President Obama declared… times are different enough that we need to "retire all dirty fuel sources entirely within the next 28 years." And those who bother looking forward should concede: He may be right (no matter how radical you believe his comments). Furthermore … almost
